task_completion.description: Open and read the FM handover schedule text file to identify required documents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/fm_handover_schedule.txt, showing document names like 'Fire Safety Certificate' or 'Door Maintenance Manual'.

task_completion.description: Locate and open the Document management or Project Information panel in Bonsai/BlenderBIM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai/BlenderBIM interface showing the active panel for managing IFC documents (IfcDocumentInformation or IfcDocumentReference).

task_completion.description: Create document entities for the required O&M documents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai/BlenderBIM interface showing new document entries added to the document list matching the schedule (e.g., 'Fire Safety Certificate', 'Door Maintenance Manual', 'Window Warranty Documentation', 'Structural Inspection Report').

task_completion.description: Associate the created document entities with the corresponding building element types.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai/BlenderBIM interface showing building elements (like doors, windows, walls, or slabs) selected in the 3D viewport or outliner, with documents being actively assigned to them in the properties or document assignment panel.

task_completion.description: Save the enriched IFC model to the specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog in Blender showing the file path /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_fm_handover.ifc, or a terminal/file manager confirming the file's creation.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Bonsai/BlenderBIM graphical interface to enrich the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Blender interface with Bonsai/BlenderBIM panels active, rather than just terminal commands or a text editor modifying the IFC file directly.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely selected building elements and assigned documents to them in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport or outliner shows specific elements selected while the document assignment panel is used, rather than just a bulk script execution without visual confirmation.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file after making modifications.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender interface shows the 'Save As' dialog or export process after the document entities and associations were visibly created in the UI, not just copying or renaming the original file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The FM handover schedule contains exactly 4 required documents: 'Fire Safety Certificate', 'Door Maintenance Manual', 'Window Warranty Documentation', and 'Structural Inspection Report'. The FZK-Haus model contains 13 walls, 5 doors, 11 windows, and 4 slabs. The final model must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_fm_handover.ifc'. A successful execution requires creating at least 4 document entities and associating them with at least 20 building elements in total.
